children.heading: Natural gas
children.label: 1 Natural gas
children.text: The term “natural gas” means either natural gas unmixed, or any mixture of natural and artificial gas.

children.heading: Well
children.label: 2 Well
children.text: The term “well” means any well for the discovery or production of natural gas, crude oil, or both.

children.label: A
children.text: the surface drilling of which began on or after ; orFebruary 19, 1977

children.label: B
children.text: the depth of which was increased, by means of drilling on or after , to a completion location which is located at least 1,000 feet below the depth of the deepest completion location of such well attained before .February 19, 1977February 19, 1977

children.heading: Old well
children.label: 4 Old well
children.text: The term “old well” means any well other than a new well.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: A General rule
children.text: The term “marker well” means any well from which natural gas was produced in commercial quantities at any time after , and before .January 1, 1970April 20, 1977

children.heading: New wells
children.label: B New wells
children.text: The term “marker well” does not include any new well under paragraph (3)(A) but includes any new well under paragraph (3)(B) if such well qualifies as a marker well under subparagraph (A) of this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: by impermeable rock or water barriers and characterized by a single natural pressure system; or

children.label: B
children.text: by lithologic or structural barriers which prevent pressure communication.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: A General rule
children.text: The term “completion location” means any subsurface location from which natural gas is being or has been produced in commercial quantities.

children.heading: Marker well
children.label: B Marker well
children.text: The term “completion location”, when used with reference to any marker well, means any subsurface location from which natural gas was produced from such well in commercial quantities after , and before .January 1, 1970April 20, 1977

children.label: A
children.text: any portion of a reservoir, as designated by the State or Federal agency having regulatory jurisdiction with respect to production from such reservoir, which will be effectively and efficiently drained by a single well;

children.label: B
children.text: any drilling unit, production unit, or comparable arrangement, designated or recognized by the State or Federal agency having jurisdiction with respect to production from the reservoir, to describe that portion of such reservoir which will be effectively and efficiently drained by a single well; or

children.label: C
children.text: if such portion of a reservoir, unit, or comparable arrangement is not specifically provided for by State law or by any action of any State or Federal agency having regulatory jurisdiction with respect to production from such reservoir, any voluntary unit agreement or other comparable arrangement applied, under local custom or practice within the locale in which such reservoir is situated, for the purpose of describing the portion of a reservoir which may be effectively and efficiently drained by a single well.

children.heading: New lease
children.label: 9 New lease
children.text: The term “new lease”, when used with respect to the Outer Continental Shelf, means a lease, entered into on or after , of submerged acreage.April 20, 1977

children.heading: Old lease
children.label: 10 Old lease
children.text: The term “old lease”, when used with respect to the Outer Continental Shelf, means any lease other than a new lease.

children.heading: New contract
children.label: 11 New contract
children.text: The term “new contract” means any contract, entered into on or after , for the first sale of natural gas which was not previously subject to an existing contract.November 9, 1978

children.heading: Rollover contract
children.label: 12 Rollover contract
children.text: The term “rollover contract” means any contract, entered into on or after , for the first sale of natural gas that was previously subject to an existing contract which expired at the end of a fixed term (not including any extension thereof taking effect on or after ) specified by the provisions of such existing contract, as such contract was in effect on , whether or not there is an identity of parties or terms with those of such existing contract.November 9, 1978November 9, 1978November 9, 1978

children.heading: Existing contract
children.label: 13 Existing contract
children.text: The term “existing contract” means any contract for the first sale of natural gas in effect on .November 8, 1978

children.heading: Successor to an existing contract
children.label: 14 Successor to an existing contract
children.text: The term “successor to an existing contract” means any contract, other than a roll­over contract, entered into on or after , for the first sale of natural gas which was previously subject to an existing contract, whether or not there is an identity of parties or terms with those of such existing contract.November 9, 1978

children.heading: Interstate pipeline
children.label: 15 Interstate pipeline
children.text: The term “interstate pipeline” means any person engaged in natural gas transportation subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission under the Natural Gas Act [ et seq.].15 U.S.C. 717

children.heading: Intrastate pipeline
children.label: 16 Intrastate pipeline
children.text: The term “intrastate pipeline” means any person engaged in natural gas transportation (not including gathering) which is not subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission under the Natural Gas Act [ et seq.] (other than any such pipeline which is not subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission solely by reason of section 1(c) of the Natural Gas Act []).15 U.S.C. 71715 U.S.C. 717(c)

children.heading: Local distribution company
children.label: 17 Local distribution company
children.text: The term “local distribution company” means any person, other than any interstate pipeline or any intrastate pipeline, engaged in the transportation, or local distribution, of natural gas and the sale of natural gas for ultimate consumption.

children.heading: Committed or dedicated to interstate commerce
children.label: 18 Committed or dedicated to interstate commerce
i
children.text: natural gas which is from the Outer Continental Shelf; and

children.label: ii
children.text: natural gas which, if sold, would be required to be sold in interstate commerce (within the meaning of the Natural Gas Act [ et seq.]) under the terms of any contract, any certificate under the Natural Gas Act, or any provision of such Act.15 U.S.C. 717

children.label: I
children.text: under section 6 of the Emergency Natural Gas Act of 1977;

children.label: II
children.text: under any limited term certificate, granted pursuant to section 7 of the Natural Gas Act [], which contains a pregrant of abandonment of service for such natural gas;15 U.S.C. 717f

children.label: III
children.text: under any emergency regulation under the second proviso of section 7(c) of the Natural Gas Act []; or15 U.S.C. 717f(c)

children.label: IV
children.text: to the user by the producer and transported under any certificate, granted pursuant to section 7(c) of the Natural Gas Act [], if such certificate was specifically granted for the transportation of that natural gas for such user;15 U.S.C. 717f(c)

children.label: ii
children.text: natural gas for which abandonment of service was granted before , under section 7 of the Natural Gas Act []; andNovember 9, 197815 U.S.C. 717f

children.label: I
children.text: neither that person, nor any affiliate thereof, had any right to explore for, develop, produce, or sell such natural gas; and

children.label: II
children.text: such natural gas was not being sold in interstate commerce (within the meaning of the Natural Gas Act [ et seq.]) for resale (other than any sale described in clause (i)(I), (II), or (III)).15 U.S.C. 717

children.heading: Certificated natural gas
children.label: 19 Certificated natural gas
children.text: The term “certificated natural gas” means natural gas transported by any interstate pipeline in a facility for which there is in effect a certificate issued under section 7(c) of the Natural Gas Act []. Such term does not include natural gas sold to the user by the producer and transported pursuant to a certificate which is specifically issued under section 7(c) of the Natural Gas Act for the transportation of that natural gas, for such user unless such natural gas is used for the generation of electricity.15 U.S.C. 717f(c)

children.heading: Sale
children.label: 20 Sale
children.text: The term “sale” means any sale, exchange, or other transfer for value.

children.label: i
children.text: to any interstate pipeline or intrastate pipeline;

children.label: ii
children.text: to any local distribution company;

children.label: iii
children.text: to any person for use by such person;

children.label: iv
children.text: which precedes any sale described in clauses (i), (ii), or (iii); and

children.label: v
children.text: which precedes or follows any sale described in clauses (i), (ii), (iii), or (iv) and is defined by the Commission as a first sale in order to prevent circumvention of any maximum lawful price established under this chapter.

children.heading: Certain sales not included
children.label: B Certain sales not included
children.text: Clauses (i), (ii), (iii), or (iv) of subparagraph (A) shall not include the sale of any volume of natural gas by any interstate pipeline, intrastate pipeline, or local distribution company, or any affiliate thereof, unless such sale is attributable to volumes of natural gas produced by such interstate pipeline, intrastate pipeline, or local distribution company, or any affiliate thereof.

children.heading: Deliver
children.label: 22 Deliver
children.text: The term “deliver”, when used with respect to any first sale of natural gas, means the physical delivery from the seller; except that in the case of the sale of proven reserves in place to any interstate pipeline, any intrastate pipeline, any local distribution company, or any user of such natural gas, such term means the transfer of title to such reserves.

children.heading: Certificate
children.label: 23 Certificate
children.text: The term “certificate”, when used with respect to the Natural Gas Act [ et seq.], means a certificate of public convenience and necessity issued under such Act.15 U.S.C. 717

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 24 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.

children.heading: Federal agency
children.label: 25 Federal agency
children.text: The term “Federal agency” has the same meaning as given such term in .section 105 of title 5

children.heading: Person
children.label: 26 Person
children.text: The term “person” includes the United States, any State, and any political subdivision, agency, or instrumentality of the foregoing.

children.heading: Affiliate
children.label: 27 Affiliate
children.text: The term “affiliate”, when used in relation to any person, means another person which controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, such person.

children.heading: Electric utility
children.label: 28 Electric utility
children.text: The term “electric utility” means any person to the extent such person is engaged in the business of the generation of electricity and sale, directly or indirectly, of electricity to the public.

children.heading: Mcf
children.label: 29 Mcf
children.text: The term “Mcf”, when used with respect to natural gas, means 1,000 cubic feet of natural gas measured at a pressure of 14.73 pounds per square inch (absolute) and a temperature of 60 degrees Fahrenheit.

children.heading: Btu
children.label: 30 Btu
children.text: The term “Btu” means British thermal unit.

children.heading: Month
children.label: 31 Month
children.text: The term “month” means a calendar month.

children.heading: Mile
children.label: 32 Mile
children.text: The term “mile” means a statute mile of 5,280 feet.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 33 United States
children.text: The term “United States” means the several States and includes the Outer Continental Shelf.

children.heading: State
children.label: 34 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States and the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Outer Continental Shelf
children.label: 35 Outer Continental Shelf
children.text: The term “Outer Continental Shelf” has the same meaning as such term has under .section 1331(a) of title 43

children.heading: Prudhoe Bay Unit of Alaska
children.label: 36 Prudhoe Bay Unit of Alaska
children.text: The term “Prudhoe Bay Unit of Alaska” means the geographic area subject to the voluntary unit agreement approved by the Commissioner of the Department of Natural Resources of the State of Alaska on , and referred to as the “affected area” in Conservation Order No. 145 of the Alaska Oil and Gas Conservation Committee, Division of Oil and Gas Conservation, Department of Natural Resources of the State of Alaska, as such order was in effect on , and determined without regard to any adjustments in the description of the affected area permitted to be made under such order.June 2, 1977June 1, 1977

children.heading: Antitrust laws
children.label: 37 Antitrust laws
children.text: The term “Federal antitrust laws” means the Sherman Act ( et seq.), the Clayton Act (, 13, 14–19, 20, 21, 22–27), the Federal Trade Commission Act ( et seq.), sections 73 and 74 of the Wilson Tariff Act (), and the Act of , chapter 592 (, 13a, 13b, and 21a).15 U.S.C. 115 U.S.C. 1215 U.S.C. 4115 U.S.C. 8–915 U.S.C. 13June 19, 1936
